seoTitle: How to Add and Set Up Credentials in n8n
metaDescription: Learn the general process for adding new credentials in n8n so your nodes can securely authenticate with external apps and services.
intro: Most n8n nodes that connect to an outside app or service need a credential entry to authenticate securely, and setting one up follows a consistent pattern across the platform. This guide keeps things general, covering how to open the credentials manager, create a new entry, and attach it to a node.

steps.heading: Open the credentials manager
steps.body: From your n8n dashboard or a specific node, find the option to create a new credential.

steps.heading: Choose the credential type
steps.body: Select the service you're connecting to, since this determines what fields you'll need to fill in.

steps.heading: Enter your authentication details
steps.body: Provide the required keys, tokens, or login information for that service.

steps.heading: Save the credential
steps.body: Confirm and save so the credential becomes available to attach to relevant nodes.

steps.heading: Attach it to a node and test
steps.body: Select your saved credential inside the node's settings, then run a test to confirm it authenticates correctly.

faq.question: Where do I add new credentials in n8n?
faq.answer: You can create a credential either from the main credentials section of your dashboard or directly from within a node that requires one.

faq.question: Are n8n credentials stored securely?
faq.answer: Credentials are kept separate from your workflow logic inside n8n's dedicated credential manager.

faq.question: Can I reuse one credential across multiple workflows?
faq.answer: Yes, a saved credential can be attached to any node that needs it across different workflows.

faq.question: What should I do if a credential stops authenticating?
faq.answer: Check whether the underlying token or key has expired or been revoked, and update the credential with fresh details.
